page: Microsoft Azure E8_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the Ev4 server family. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The processor includes a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache. This instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage for persistent disk operations.

Performance benchmarks place the E8_v4 in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The instance achieved a single-core stress-ng score of 1411.28 and a multi-core score of 7428.61. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 414.9 GB/sec and a large block uncached read speed of 49.73 GB/sec, alongside a write speed of 34.64 GB/sec. In database-related workloads, the server processed 6.96 million Redis SET operations per second and scored 2918.93 in PassMark database operations. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and LLM inference tasks, particularly with larger models like the 7B parameter model, show weaker relative performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E8_v4 offers a high-density memory configuration relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive parallel CPU processing or local NVMe storage. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces overhead for workloads focused purely on memory capacity. This instance is well-suited for relational database servers, caching layers such as Redis, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E8_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance from the Ev4 family, featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a single-core Geekbench score of 1237.0 and a multi-core score of 4762.0. Memory bandwidth is solid, reaching 414.9 GB/sec for cached small blocks, and database performance is steady with 6.96 million Redis SET operations per second. Given its high memory density and moderate compute capabilities, the E8_v4 is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise application hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor, and 64.0 GB of RAM. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads without local storage or GPU requirements.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8_v4 is a memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, designed for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for handling database, caching, and enterprise application workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores
64.0 GB of system memory providing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Delivers 6.96 million Redis SET operations per second in database benchmarks
Suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications
